data.title: As a DSO user reading configuration documentation, I can find the preplanning.interactive key documented with per-checkpoint behavior and upgrade migration guidance
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a `preplanning.interactive` entry to `plugins/dso/docs/CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md` covering the key's description, accepted values, default behavior, per-checkpoint non-interactive behavior table, and a migration note for existing projects.

**Why**: Without documentation, operators cannot discover the key exists, understand how each checkpoint behaves non-interactively, or know how to migrate projects that relied on always-interactive preplanning behavior.

**Scope**:
- IN: `plugins/dso/docs/CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md` (new key entry with per-checkpoint behavior table and migration note)
- OUT: Schema changes (covered in S1); preplanning SKILL.md behavioral changes (S2); onboarding changes (S4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md` contains a `preplanning.interactive` entry with: accepted values (true/false), default behavior (non-interactive when absent), and a table mapping each of the 7 interactive checkpoints to their non-interactive fallback behavior
  ← Satisfies: "`CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md` and `workflow-config-schema.json` document `preplanning.interactive` with description, accepted values (true/false), default (non-interactive when absent), per-checkpoint behavior table"
- When this story is complete, the documentation includes a migration note for existing projects that relied on always-interactive preplanning behavior, explaining that upgrading requires explicitly setting `preplanning.interactive=true` to preserve the old behavior
  ← Satisfies: "`CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md` ... migration note for existing projects upgrading from always-interactive behavior"
- When this story is complete, the documentation notes that `preplanning.interactive` must be registered in `validate-config.sh` KNOWN_KEYS when adding the key to a new host project (noting that DSO's own registration was completed in S1)
  ← Satisfies: adversarial finding — operators need to know about KNOWN_KEYS registration requirement

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Documentation-only story — exempt from TDD Done-of-Done unit test requirement; observable outcomes focus on doc content, not code coverage
- [Reliability] The migration note is important for the "breaking change for existing users" scenario: absent key = non-interactive is a silent behavior change for all current projects on upgrade

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
